title: Pre-op obesity anesthesia review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.
red_flags.rationale: The ASA section explicitly identifies obesity as a modifier requiring clinician assessment in fasting and aspiration-risk context; it does not create fasting or medicine instructions.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports noting pregnancy, obesity, diabetes, reflux, obstruction, emergency, and difficult-airway context as assessment qualifiers only.

source.title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration

medication.text: Supports previous anesthesia records and alerts plus age, comorbidity, medicines, allergy, surgery, pain, cognition, frailty, nutrition, lifestyle, psychological, functional, and chronic-pain risk assessment.
medication.rationale: The RCoA section supports documenting interval change, function, comorbidity, nutrition, lifestyle, medicines, airway and cardiopulmonary assessment when performed.
medication.evidence_extract: Supports previous anesthesia records and alerts plus age, comorbidity, medicines, allergy, surgery, pain, cognition, frailty, nutrition, lifestyle, psychological, functional, and chronic-pain risk assessment.

source.title: Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services: Chapter 2 — Guidelines for the Provision of Anaesthesia Services for the Perioperative Care of Elective and Urgent Care Patients 2025

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 3.6 and 4.1–4.4 — previous records and risk domains

sources_used.title: Practice Guidelines for Preoperative Fasting and the Use of Pharmacologic Agents to Reduce the Risk of Pulmonary Aspiration

source_limitations: Height, weight, BMI, distribution, OSA, airway, reflux, diabetes, cardiorespiratory disease, mobility, equipment needs, venous-thromboembolism risk, surgery type, fasting, medicine, technique, monitoring, disposition, and follow-up remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
